title: narcissus festalis var. rugilobus
intro: Narcissus festalis var. rugilobus, also known as the Mediterranean Daffodil or Wild Narcissus, is a perennial plant species in the Amaryllidaceae family, characterized by its delicate white flowers and narrow leaves. This plant is traditionally used in herbal medicine due to its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which can be used to treat various health issues such as wounds, fever, and respiratory problems. The medicinal properties of Narcissus festalis var. rugilobus have been found to provide health benefits, including reducing inflammation, relieving pain, and protecting against infection. The plant can be prepared as an herbal remedy through infusions, decoctions, or tinctures, often used to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs.
